ORAL & MAXILLOFACIAL SURGERY
DESIGN GUIDELINE
SECTION 1 — HERO
Layout
Bố cục 2 cột.
Bên trái:
● Headline
● Intro copy
● CTA buttons
Bên phải:
● Hero image
Desktop:
50/50 layout.
Mobile:
Text trước.
Ảnh bên dưới.
Website Copy
Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery
Some dental conditions may require surgical treatment.
Whether related to wisdom teeth, infection, cysts, or other oral conditions, the first step is understanding if surgery is actually needed.
Primary Media
Ưu tiên hình ảnh thật tại EMIS.
Ví dụ:
● Bác sĩ tư vấn cho bệnh nhân
● Bác sĩ xem X-ray hoặc CBCT
Có thể sử dụng:
● Màn hình CBCT
● X-ray review
● Hình ảnh tư vấn thực tế
SECTION 2 — COULD THIS APPLY TO YOU?
Layout
Checklist section.
Desktop:
● 2 cột
Mobile:
● 1 cột
Website Copy
✓ Impacted wisdom teeth
✓ Recurrent pain, swelling, or infection
✓ Teeth that cannot be removed through routine extraction
✓ Jaw cysts or other oral lesions
✓ Teeth affecting orthodontic or restorative treatment
These are some of the situations where patients often wonder if they need surgery.
Primary Media
Không cần hình ảnh lớn.
Sử dụng:
● Checklist
● Icon cards
● Symptom cards
Supporting Media
Có thể minh họa nhẹ:
● Răng khôn mọc lệch
● Nhiễm trùng
● Nang xương
● Răng ngầm
SECTION 3 — HOW DO DOCTORS DETERMINE?
Layout
2 cột.
Bên trái:
● Nội dung
Bên phải:
● Evaluation graphic
Website Copy
The clinical team may evaluate:
✓ Symptoms
✓ Clinical examination
✓ X-rays or CBCT imaging
✓ Position of teeth or lesions
✓ Treatment goals
This helps provide greater clarity before any treatment decisions are made.
Primary Media
Infographic đơn giản.
Ví dụ:
Symptoms
●
Clinical Examination
●
X-rays / CBCT
●
Treatment Goals
↓
Clinical Recommendation
Supporting Media
● X-ray review
● CBCT review
● Consultation images
SECTION 4 — WHAT DOES THE PROCESS TYPICALLY LOOK LIKE?
Layout
Timeline section.
Desktop:
Timeline ngang.
Mobile:
Timeline dọc.
Website Copy
Assessment
↓
Diagnosis
↓
Treatment Planning
↓
Procedure
↓
Healing & Follow-Up
The exact process depends on the condition being treated and the complexity of the procedure.
Primary Media
Timeline graphic.
Thiết kế tối giản.
Dễ scan.
Supporting Media
Có thể sử dụng:
● Consultation
● Treatment planning
● Follow-up visit
SECTION 5 — FOR OVERSEAS PATIENTS
Layout
2 cột.
Bên trái:
● Nội dung
Bên phải:
● International patient visual
Website Copy
Many overseas Vietnamese and international patients send X-rays before traveling to Vietnam.
This helps the team review the situation, discuss possible next steps, and support treatment planning before the trip.
Treatment in Vietnam may also make it possible to address oral surgical needs that may have felt difficult or expensive to pursue at home.
Primary Media
International patient journey.
Ví dụ:
Send X-ray
↓
Review
↓
Travel
↓
Treatment
↓
Follow-Up
Supporting Media
● Bệnh nhân trao đổi từ xa
● Điều phối viên hỗ trợ
● Hành trình điều trị quốc tế
SECTION 6 — REAL PATIENT CASES
Layout
Featured case + supporting cases.
Desktop:
● 1 case nổi bật
● 3–6 case phụ
Mobile:
● Carousel
Website Copy
Explore how patients with wisdom teeth, infection, cysts, and other oral surgical concerns were treated at EMIS.
CTA
View Oral Surgery Cases

Case thực tế.
Ưu tiên:
● Before–After imaging
● X-ray

● Clinical documentation

Initial Situation
↓
Assessment

↓

SECTION 7 — CTA
Layout
Centered CTA section.
Nhiều khoảng trắng.
Thiết kế đơn giản.
Website Copy
Not Sure What The Next Step Should Be?
If you already have an X-ray or CBCT scan, you can send it for a preliminary review.
Primary CTA
Send Your X-ray
Secondary CTA
Discuss Your Situation
Note
Final treatment recommendations require an in-person examination and appropriate diagnostic evaluation.
Primary Media
Có thể sử dụng:
● X-ray icon
● Consultation icon
● Treatment planning icon
Supporting Media
Simple process:
Send X-ray
↓
Preliminary Review
↓
Next Steps
